flora-rustica-1792.about:
## About this volume

**Flora Rustica** is an illustrated botanical work by **Thomas Martyn** (1735–1825), Regius Professor of Botany at Cambridge, issued in London by the botanical painter Frederick P. Nodder from 1792. The title announces its purpose plainly: to give *accurate figures of such plants as are either useful or injurious in husbandry.*

Where the fashionable florilegia of the age chased ornament, Martyn's flora is a working book for the countryside. Each plate is paired with a short English description covering:

- appearance and Linnaean classification,
- range and preferred soil,
- and — the point of the whole enterprise — its *use or harm* to the farmer.

> The design of this work is to figure and describe such plants as the farmer is chiefly interested in knowing; whether because they are useful to him in husbandry, or because they are hurtful to his cattle, or troublesome weeds among his crops.

### Why it matters

The book sits at the meeting point of Linnaean science and rural practice. Its combination of precise illustration, taxonomic description, and applied commentary makes it an early ancestor of the modern weed-identification manual, and a valuable record of which plants an eighteenth-century farmer prized and which he dug out.

The scan here is a short *sample* of the plates and letterpress, prepared for the reading-room demonstration.

the-monthly-flora-1846.about:
## About this volume

**The Monthly Flora** was issued in parts through 1846 by Lewis & Brown of London — a serial arrangement, one gathering of hand-coloured plates and description delivered each month, that let a subscriber assemble a complete flora over the course of a year.

Serial botanical works of this kind were popular in the early Victorian period: affordable in instalments, and current enough that a plate could be timed to the month its subject came into flower. The present copy is catalogued from a bound run of the monthly parts.

histoire-naturelle-de-vegetaux-1846.about:
## À propos de cet ouvrage

**Histoire Naturelle des Végétaux** de **Édouard Spach** parut à Paris chez la Librairie Encyclopédique de Roret dans le cadre de la célèbre *Suite à Buffon*. L'ouvrage propose une classification systématique des plantes phanérogames, accompagnée de descriptions détaillées des genres et des espèces.

Il s'agit d'une œuvre de référence de la botanique française du XIXe siècle, à mi-chemin entre le traité savant et le manuel de détermination.
